q: Auto Loader schema evolution: what are the four cloudFiles.schemaEvolutionMode values and which do you use in production?
a: Auto Loader infers schema by sampling the first 50 GB or 1,000 files and persists it to cloudFiles.schemaLocation (a _schemas directory at that path). When the source schema changes, the behavior is controlled by cloudFiles.schemaEvolutionMode: addNewColumns (the default when no explicit schema is provided) restarts the stream with an UnknownFieldException when a new column is detected, incorporating the wider schema on restart — pair this with a Jobs retry to self-heal; rescue captures new columns silently in _rescued_data without ever failing the stream — use this when you can tolerate deferred handling of new fields; failOnNewColumns stops the stream permanently and requires a human to update the schema or remove the file — use this when an unexpected column is a pipeline contract violation; none ignores all new columns entirely, dropping them. The production default for unattended pipelines is addNewColumns with auto-restart, because it surfaces new columns immediately rather than silently losing them. If you can't afford a restart gap, rescue is the safe fallback. Never use none when data loss is unacceptable.
fj: I'd just leave schemaEvolutionMode at the default and not set schemaLocation — Auto Loader will figure it out.
fs: schemaLocation is required to persist the inferred schema across restarts. Without it, Auto Loader re-infers from scratch every run, which is slow and inconsistent. The default addNewColumns mode fails (and auto-restarts) on new columns — that's intentional, but you must configure a Job retry policy or the stream stays down.

code.label: rescue mode — new cols captured silently
lines: # New columns land in _rescued_data, not a separate column
